job_description:
About 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, serving mid-market and enterprise clients in the US and UK. Our expertise spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernization, and operational transformation.

Role Overview

This is a senior B2B content specialist role focused on operationalizing and enforcing the company's written voice across all content. It is not a content management or social media role. The position demands technical credibility, commercial relevance, and a clear point of view in content tailored for technical leaders and business decision-makers (CTOs, IT Directors, Founders, COOs, etc.).

Key Responsibilities

  • Own all long-form content: pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers
  • Write content in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing
  • Audit and enforce brand standards across all published content
  • Create clear content briefs executable by junior team members
  • Act as the content quality gatekeeper for all publications
  • Use AI tools as a productivity layer, not a primary author

What You Won't Own

  • SEO keyword selection (handled by SEO Specialist)
  • Social media scheduling (managed by a separate team)
  • Visual design (owned by Visual Designer)
  • Distribution strategy (decided by Marketing & Growth Lead)
  • ICP definition (you write for the defined ICP)

Requirements

  • 3-5 years of B2B content experience (5-6 years preferred)
  • Proven Google rankings for written content
  • Experience writing in executive/leadership voices
  • Working-level SEO understanding (search intent, content alignment)
  • AI writing tool proficiency with critical thinking
  • Demonstrable content impact on traffic, leads, or engagement
  • Background in tech/SaaS/professional services preferred

Writing & Mindset

  • Clear point-of-view writing, not neutral summaries
  • Ability to simplify complex technical concepts without losing credibility
  • Concise, no-filler writing style
  • Comfort with being edited and accepting feedback
  • Genuine curiosity about AI, technology, and business operations
  • Fast-paced execution (no 3-day draft delays)

What We're Not Looking For

  • Portfolios of generic "Top 10 Tips" or trend summaries
  • Writers unable to quantify content performance
  • Candidates without technical/business audience experience
  • Self-directed writers who ignore briefs
  • Writers who treat AI tools as primary authors

Benefits

  • Growth-driven environment with leadership opportunities
  • Continuous innovation and upskilling

Contact: Apply by sharing your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com
summary: Senior B2B content specialist ensuring consistent, high-quality written output for technical and business leaders, with a focus on SEO-aligned, commercially relevant content.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

About 2Base Technologies 2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the center of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

This is a strategic and technical role. It is not an SEO support role, a blogging role with keyword research added on, or a task-execution position within a larger team. If that distinction matters to you, read on.

What You Will Own - The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline - Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences - Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking - On-page SEO across all published pages - Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data - Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification - The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages - SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing - Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards - SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own - Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages - Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard - Paid search: that is a separate role - Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner - Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest, able to diagnose and adjust independently - Collaborative but not passive

What We Are Not Looking For - SEO practitioners focused solely on informational content - Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task - Candidates who cannot explain high-intent vs. informational keyword targeting - Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools - Candidates with no exposure to US/UK search behavior

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Lead organic search strategy for US/UK markets, building scalable systems for high-intent content and technical SEO. Requires 3-5 years of commercial SEO experience with programmatic architecture expertise.

We are seeking a detail-oriented Business Analyst with 1–2 years of experience to gather, analyze, and document business requirements for web and mobile applications. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience in requirement elicitation, process mapping, and user story creation, and will work closely with stakeholders, product managers, and development teams to deliver scalable, high-quality software solutions that align with business objectives.

Key Responsibilities

  1. Requirement Gathering and Analysis:
  2. Collaborating with stakeholders (clients, product owners, etc.) to understand their needs.
  3. Translating business requirements into detailed functional specifications for the development team.

  4. Organising workshops, interviews, or surveys to collect business requirements.

  5. Process Mapping and Documentation:

  6. Creating flowcharts, diagrams, or process maps to visually represent business workflows and system processes.

  7. Documenting the As-Is (current state) and To-Be (future state) processes for stakeholders and development teams.

  8. Liaison Between Business and Development Teams:

  9. Acting as a bridge between non-technical stakeholders and technical teams to ensure clear communication.

  10. Explaining technical details to non-technical users in simple terms and ensuring developers understand business goals.

  11. Creating User Stories and Use Cases:

  12. Writing detailed user stories or use cases that define how end-users will interact with the software.

  13. Clarifying acceptance criteria for features and functionalities.

  14. Facilitating Meetings and Sprint Planning:

  15. Organising and leading requirement review meetings, sprint planning sessions, and retrospectives.

  16. Ensuring alignment between the business and technical teams during the software development lifecycle.

  17. Assisting in User Acceptance Testing (UAT):

  18. Coordinating and facilitating UAT by creating test scenarios, helping users test the software, and ensuring it meets their expectations.

  19. Identifying and addressing any defects or issues raised during UAT.

  20. Change Management and Impact Analysis:

  21. Performing impact analysis on any changes requested by the client or stakeholders.

  22. Communicating the risks, benefits, and effects of changes on existing systems and processes.

  23. Market Research and Competitor Analysis:

  24. Conducting market research and competitor analysis to understand industry trends and ensure the product meets market needs.

Preferred Skills

  1. Understanding of Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC):

  2. The BA will learn various methodologies such as Agile, Scrum, or Waterfall and understand how different teams interact during the SDLC.

  3. Stakeholder Management:

  4. Building relationships with key stakeholders and learning how to effectively manage their expectations and communicate requirements clearly.

  5. Requirements Elicitation Techniques:

  6. Mastering techniques for gathering and defining requirements, such as interviews, workshops, brainstorming sessions, and creating wireframes or mockups.

  7. Documentation Skills:

  8. Have good writing skills, clear and concise documentation (e.g., Business Requirement Documents (BRD), Functional Requirement Specifications (FRS), and user stories).

  9. Tools and Software:

  10. Have proficiency in tools such as Jira, Confluence, Microsoft Visio, Balsamiq, Trello, and wire framing or prototyping software.

  11. Communication and Presentation:

  12. Communication skills to convey ideas to both technical and non-technical audiences.

  13. Have presentation skills in findings, reports, and project updates in meetings.

  14. Problem-Solving and Analytical Thinking:

  15. Ability to quickly understand problems, analyse them, and propose practical solutions that align with business objectives.

  16. Handling Ambiguity and Change:

  17. Learning to work in fast-paced environments with constantly changing requirements and priorities.

  18. Adapting to changes quickly and being able to assess the impact of changes on projects.

  19. Testing and Quality Assurance (QA):

  20. Understanding of how software testing works and how to coordinate user acceptance testing (UAT) with the end-users.

  21. Ensuring that all requirements are tested and verified before the software is released.

  22. Business Domain Knowledge:

  23. Acquiring knowledge about the specific industry or domain the business operates in (e.g., finance, healthcare, retail) to make informed decisions and provide valuable insights to the team.

Why You’ll Love Working With Us

  • Meaningful Work with Global Impact: Contribute to enterprise platforms used by world-class organizations such as Philips, Hitachi, Pfizer, and other global leaders—helping them improve how they operate, lead, and grow.
  • People-First, Collaborative Culture: Your ideas are heard, your work is valued, and you’re supported by a team built on trust and respect.
  • Purpose-Driven Technology: Build practical, thoughtful products that solve real operational and leadership challenges.
  • Growth & Ownership: Learn continuously, take ownership of your work, and grow your career in a supportive environment.
  • Global Exposure, Human Connection: Work with diverse teams and enterprise clients worldwide—while feeling connected and supported.
  • Quality, Security & Trust: Our platforms and processes are backed by internationally recognised ISO certifications, reflecting our strong commitment to quality, data security, and operational reliability. This means you work in an environment built on high standards and trust.

Build a Strong Future Together

At LTS, we don’t just deliver solutions—we build long-term relationships. Together, we create a strong, sustainable future for our people, our clients, and our organization. From continuous learning and career development opportunities to meaningful ownership in your work, we support you at every stage of your journey. Our people benefit from a supportive work environment, competitive benefits, and a culture that values balance, trust, and long-term success.

At LTS, your growth is our growth—and when our people succeed, everything we build becomes stronger.
